The room stretches endlessly, mirrored walls reflecting a thousand fragmented versions of yourself. It's quiet here.
You take a step forward. The sound of your footsteps echoes, yet the distance seems unchanged.
A sudden shift in the light catches your eye. The reflections waver, as if the glass itself is breathing.
You realize you aren't just walking through a room. You're walking through a memory that refuses to settle.
There is a quiet power in simply moving forward, even when the destination is obscured by your own reflection.
